topic: Bootstrap-to-Scale Infrastructure — How Artists Build Institutions Without Selling Out (Bloom Analysis)
content_preview: Bloom/growth analysis of the Omni-Dromenon-Engine project. Research across 15+ case studies (Radiohead, NIN, Cirque du Soleil, Burning Man, Wooster Group, Blast Theory, Fugazi, Linux, Kubernetes, Apache Foundation) extracting bootstrap-to-scale patterns. Key findings: 7-15 year timeline to institutional scale, critical 3-7 year governance window, funding inflection points $40K-$1.5M, technology choices encoding artistic possibility, documentation practices distinguishing survivors from one-time successes. Includes financial data tables, timeline patterns, community governance models (meritocracy vs benevolent dictatorship vs collective), and revenue stack analysis. Framed from zero-budget adjunct-instructor starting position targeting touring-ballet/Hamilton scale.
